Module com.example.cincuentazo
Package com.example.cincuentazo.controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
com.example.cincuentazo.controller.GameController
All Implemented Interfaces:
CardClickListener, javafx.fxml.Initializable
public class GameController extends Object implements javafx.fxml.Initializable, CardClickListener
Controller for the main game view of Cincuentazo.

This controller connects the GameLogic engine with the visual board. It deals the initial hands, renders the cards, and handles the human player's interaction through mouse and keyboard events. Machine turns (with timers) are added in the concurrency phase.

    Inner class that handles keyboard shortcuts during the game.

    • GameController

      public GameController()

  • Method Details

    • setMachineOpponents

      public void setMachineOpponents(int machineOpponents)
      Sets the number of machine opponents and starts the game.
      Parameters:
      machineOpponents - the number of machine opponents (1, 2 or 3)

    • initialize

      public void initialize(URL location, ResourceBundle resources)
      Initialization callback. Wires the back button and keyboard handling.
      Specified by:
      initialize in interface javafx.fxml.Initializable
      Parameters:
      location - the FXML location (unused)
      resources - the resource bundle (unused)

    • startGame

      private void startGame()
      Builds the players, sets up the game engine, deals the cards and renders the initial board.

    • refreshBoard

      private void refreshBoard()
      Redraws the entire board to reflect the current model state.

    • renderTable

      private void renderTable()
      Renders the top card currently on the table.

    • renderHumanHand

      private void renderHumanHand()
      Renders the human player's hand face-up, attaching a mouse adapter to each card so it can be clicked.

    • renderMachineHands

      private void renderMachineHands()
      Renders the machine players' hands face-down in their panes. Side opponents (left and right) stack their cards vertically to fit the narrow lateral space, while the top opponent stacks them horizontally.

    • onCardClicked

      public void onCardClicked(Card card)
      Invoked when the human player clicks on one of their cards.

      Reacts to the human player clicking one of their cards: validates and plays the move, draws a replacement, and then triggers the machine players' turns.

      Specified by:
      onCardClicked in interface CardClickListener
      Parameters:
      card - the card that was clicked

    • chooseCardValue

      private int chooseCardValue(Card card)
      Chooses the most convenient value for a card. For an Ace, it picks the highest value that does not exceed the table limit; otherwise it uses the card's fixed value.
      Parameters:
      card - the card to evaluate
      Returns:
      the chosen value to play the card with

    • installKeyboardHandler

      private void installKeyboardHandler()
      Installs the keyboard handler on the scene once it is available.

    • processNextTurn

      private void processNextTurn()
      Processes the next turn. If the game is over, it announces the winner. If it is the human's turn, it waits for the human's click. If it is a machine's turn, it schedules the machine's move on a background thread.

    • playMachineTurn

      private void playMachineTurn(Player machine)
      Executes a machine player's turn: chooses a move, plays it (or eliminates the player if no move is possible), draws a card after a short delay, and then advances to the next turn.
      Parameters:
      machine - the machine player taking its turn

    • announceWinner

      private void announceWinner()
      Announces the winner of the game and ends the interaction.
